speaker: KING
text: Welcome, dear Rosencrantz and Guildenstern.

speaker: KING
text: Moreover that we much did long to see you,

speaker: KING
text: The need we have to use you did provoke

speaker: KING
text: Our hasty sending. Something have you heard

speaker: KING
text: Of Hamlet’s transformation, so call it,

speaker: KING
text: Sith nor th’ exterior nor the inward man

speaker: KING
text: Resembles that it was. What it should be,

speaker: KING
text: More than his father’s death, that thus hath put him

speaker: KING
text: So much from th’ understanding of himself

speaker: KING
text: I cannot dream of. I entreat you both

speaker: KING
text: That, being of so young days brought up with him

speaker: KING
text: And sith so neighbored to his youth and havior,

speaker: KING
text: That you vouchsafe your rest here in our court

speaker: KING
text: Some little time, so by your companies

speaker: KING
text: To draw him on to pleasures, and to gather

speaker: KING
text: So much as from occasion you may glean,

speaker: KING
text: Whether aught to us unknown afflicts him thus

speaker: KING
text: That, opened, lies within our remedy.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Good gentlemen, he hath much talked of you,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And sure I am two men there is not living

speaker: QUEEN
text: To whom he more adheres. If it will please you

speaker: QUEEN
text: To show us so much gentry and goodwill

speaker: QUEEN
text: As to expend your time with us awhile

speaker: QUEEN
text: For the supply and profit of our hope,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Your visitation shall receive such thanks

speaker: QUEEN
text: As fits a king’s remembrance.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Both your Majesties

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Might, by the sovereign power you have of us,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Put your dread pleasures more into command

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Than to entreaty.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: But we both obey,

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: And here give up ourselves in the full bent

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: To lay our service freely at your feet,

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: To be commanded.

speaker: KING
text: Thanks, Rosencrantz and gentle Guildenstern.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Thanks, Guildenstern and gentle Rosencrantz.

speaker: QUEEN
text: And I beseech you instantly to visit

speaker: QUEEN
text: My too much changèd son.—Go, some of you,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And bring these gentlemen where Hamlet is.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Heavens make our presence and our practices

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Pleasant and helpful to him!

speaker: QUEEN
text: Ay, amen!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Th’ ambassadors from Norway, my good lord,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Are joyfully returned.

speaker: KING
text: Thou still hast been the father of good news.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Have I, my lord? I assure my good liege

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I hold my duty as I hold my soul,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Both to my God and to my gracious king,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And I do think, or else this brain of mine

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Hunts not the trail of policy so sure

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As it hath used to do, that I have found

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The very cause of Hamlet’s lunacy.

speaker: KING
text: O, speak of that! That do I long to hear.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Give first admittance to th’ ambassadors.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: My news shall be the fruit to that great feast.

speaker: KING
text: Thyself do grace to them and bring them in.

speaker: KING
text: He tells me, my dear Gertrude, he hath found

speaker: KING
text: The head and source of all your son’s distemper.

speaker: QUEEN
text: I doubt it is no other but the main—

speaker: QUEEN
text: His father’s death and our o’erhasty marriage.

speaker: KING
text: Well, we shall sift him.

speaker: KING
text: Welcome, my good friends.

speaker: KING
text: Say, Voltemand, what from our brother Norway?

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Most fair return of greetings and desires.

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Upon our first, he sent out to suppress

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: His nephew’s levies, which to him appeared

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: To be a preparation ’gainst the Polack,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: But, better looked into, he truly found

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: It was against your Highness. Whereat, grieved

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: That so his sickness, age, and impotence

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Was falsely borne in hand, sends out arrests

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: On Fortinbras, which he, in brief, obeys,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Receives rebuke from Norway, and, in fine,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Makes vow before his uncle never more

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: To give th’ assay of arms against your Majesty.

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Whereon old Norway, overcome with joy,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Gives him three-score thousand crowns in annual fee

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: And his commission to employ those soldiers,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: So levied as before, against the Polack,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: With an entreaty, herein further shown,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: That it might please you to give quiet pass

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: Through your dominions for this enterprise,

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: On such regards of safety and allowance

speaker: VOLTEMAND
text: As therein are set down.

speaker: KING
text: It likes us well,

speaker: KING
text: And, at our more considered time, we’ll read,

speaker: KING
text: Answer, and think upon this business.

speaker: KING
text: Meantime, we thank you for your well-took labor.

speaker: KING
text: Go to your rest. At night we’ll feast together.

speaker: KING
text: Most welcome home!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This business is well ended.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: My liege, and madam, to expostulate

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What majesty should be, what duty is,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Why day is day, night night, and time is time

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Were nothing but to waste night, day, and time.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Therefore, since brevity is the soul of wit,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And tediousness the limbs and outward flourishes,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I will be brief. Your noble son is mad.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: “Mad” call I it, for, to define true madness,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What is ’t but to be nothing else but mad?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But let that go.

speaker: QUEEN
text: More matter with less art.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Madam, I swear I use no art at all.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That he’s mad, ’tis true; ’tis true ’tis pity,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And pity ’tis ’tis true—a foolish figure,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But farewell it, for I will use no art.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Mad let us grant him then, and now remains

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That we find out the cause of this effect,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Or, rather say, the cause of this defect,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: For this effect defective comes by cause.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Thus it remains, and the remainder thus.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Perpend.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I have a daughter (have while she is mine)

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Who, in her duty and obedience, mark,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Hath given me this. Now gather and surmise.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To the celestial, and my soul’s idol, the

speaker: POLONIUS
text: most beautified Ophelia—

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That’s an ill phrase, a vile phrase; “beautified” is a

speaker: POLONIUS
text: vile phrase. But you shall hear. Thus:

speaker: QUEEN
text: Came this from Hamlet to her?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Good madam, stay awhile. I will be faithful.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Doubt thou the stars are fire,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Doubt that the sun doth move,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Doubt truth to be a liar,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But never doubt I love.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: O dear Ophelia, I am ill at these numbers. I have not

speaker: POLONIUS
text: art to reckon my groans, but that I love thee best, O

speaker: POLONIUS
text: most best, believe it. Adieu.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Thine evermore, most dear lady, whilst

speaker: POLONIUS
text: this machine is to him, Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This, in obedience, hath my daughter shown me,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And more above, hath his solicitings,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As they fell out by time, by means, and place,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: All given to mine ear.

speaker: KING
text: But how hath she received his love?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What do you think of me?

speaker: KING
text: As of a man faithful and honorable.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I would fain prove so. But what might you think,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: When I had seen this hot love on the wing

speaker: POLONIUS
text: (As I perceived it, I must tell you that,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Before my daughter told me), what might you,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Or my dear Majesty your queen here, think,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: If I had played the desk or table-book

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Or given my heart a winking, mute and dumb,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Or looked upon this love with idle sight?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What might you think? No, I went round to work,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And my young mistress thus I did bespeak:

speaker: POLONIUS
text: “Lord Hamlet is a prince, out of thy star.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This must not be.” And then I prescripts gave her,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That she should lock herself from his resort,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Admit no messengers, receive no tokens;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Which done, she took the fruits of my advice,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And he, repelled (a short tale to make),

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Fell into a sadness, then into a fast,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Thence to a watch, thence into a weakness,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Thence to a lightness, and, by this declension,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Into the madness wherein now he raves

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And all we mourn for.

speaker: KING
text: Do you think ’tis this?

speaker: QUEEN
text: It may be, very like.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Hath there been such a time (I would fain know that)

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That I have positively said “’Tis so,”

speaker: POLONIUS
text: When it proved otherwise?

speaker: KING
text: Not that I know.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Take this from this, if this be otherwise.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: If circumstances lead me, I will find

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Where truth is hid, though it were hid, indeed,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Within the center.

speaker: KING
text: How may we try it further?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You know sometimes he walks four hours together

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Here in the lobby.

speaker: QUEEN
text: So he does indeed.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: At such a time I’ll loose my daughter to him.

speaker: POLONIUS
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                King
                .
                 
              Be you and I behind an arras then.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Mark the encounter. If he love her not,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And be not from his reason fall’n thereon,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Let me be no assistant for a state,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But keep a farm and carters.

speaker: KING
text: We will try it.

speaker: QUEEN
text: But look where sadly the poor wretch comes reading.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Away, I do beseech you both, away.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I’ll board him presently. O, give me leave.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: How does my good Lord Hamlet?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Well, God-a-mercy.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Do you know me, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Excellent well. You are a fishmonger.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Not I, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then I would you were so honest a man.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Honest, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, sir. To be honest, as this world goes, is to

speaker: HAMLET
text: be one man picked out of ten thousand.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That’s very true, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: For if the sun breed maggots in a dead

speaker: HAMLET
text: dog, being a good kissing carrion—Have you a

speaker: HAMLET
text: daughter?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I have, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let her not walk i’ th’ sun. Conception is a

speaker: HAMLET
text: blessing, but, as your daughter may conceive,

speaker: HAMLET
text: friend, look to ’t.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: How say you by that? Still harping on

speaker: POLONIUS
text: my daughter. Yet he knew me not at first; he said I

speaker: POLONIUS
text: was a fishmonger. He is far gone. And truly, in my

speaker: POLONIUS
text: youth, I suffered much extremity for love, very near

speaker: POLONIUS
text: this. I’ll speak to him again.—What do you read, my

speaker: POLONIUS
text: lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Words, words, words.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What is the matter, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Between who?

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I mean the matter that you read, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Slanders, sir; for the satirical rogue says here

speaker: HAMLET
text: that old men have gray beards, that their faces are

speaker: HAMLET
text: wrinkled, their eyes purging thick amber and

speaker: HAMLET
text: plum-tree gum, and that they have a plentiful lack of

speaker: HAMLET
text: wit, together with most weak hams; all which, sir,

speaker: HAMLET
text: though I most powerfully and potently believe, yet I

speaker: HAMLET
text: hold it not honesty to have it thus set down; for

speaker: HAMLET
text: yourself, sir, shall grow old as I am, if, like a crab,

speaker: HAMLET
text: you could go backward.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Though this be madness, yet there is

speaker: POLONIUS
text: method in ’t.—Will you walk out of the air, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Into my grave?

speaker: POLONIUS
text:
Indeed, that’s out of the air.
                 
                Aside
                .
               How

speaker: POLONIUS
text: pregnant sometimes his replies are! A happiness

speaker: POLONIUS
text: that often madness hits on, which reason and

speaker: POLONIUS
text: sanity could not so prosperously be delivered of. I

speaker: POLONIUS
text: will leave him and suddenly contrive the means of

speaker: POLONIUS
text: meeting between him and my daughter.—My lord,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I will take my leave of you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: You cannot, sir, take from me anything that I

speaker: HAMLET
text: will more willingly part withal—except my life,

speaker: HAMLET
text: except my life, except my life.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Fare you well, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: These tedious old fools.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You go to seek the Lord Hamlet. There he is.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: God save you, sir.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: My honored lord.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: My most dear lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: My excellent good friends! How dost thou,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Guildenstern? Ah, Rosencrantz! Good lads, how do

speaker: HAMLET
text: you both?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: As the indifferent children of the earth.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Happy in that we are not overhappy.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: On Fortune’s cap, we are not the very button.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor the soles of her shoe?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Neither, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then you live about her waist, or in the

speaker: HAMLET
text: middle of her favors?

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Faith, her privates we.

speaker: HAMLET
text: In the secret parts of Fortune? O, most true!

speaker: HAMLET
text: She is a strumpet. What news?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: None, my lord, but that the world’s

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: grown honest.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then is doomsday near. But your news is not

speaker: HAMLET
text: true. Let me question more in particular. What

speaker: HAMLET
text: have you, my good friends, deserved at the hands of

speaker: HAMLET
text: Fortune that she sends you to prison hither?

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Prison, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Denmark’s a prison.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Then is the world one.

speaker: HAMLET
text: A goodly one, in which there are many confines,

speaker: HAMLET
text: wards, and dungeons, Denmark being one o’

speaker: HAMLET
text: th’ worst.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: We think not so, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, then, ’tis none to you, for there is

speaker: HAMLET
text: nothing either good or bad but thinking makes it

speaker: HAMLET
text: so. To me, it is a prison.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Why, then, your ambition makes it one.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: ’Tis too narrow for your mind.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O God, I could be bounded in a nutshell and

speaker: HAMLET
text: count myself a king of infinite space, were it not

speaker: HAMLET
text: that I have bad dreams.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Which dreams, indeed, are ambition,

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: for the very substance of the ambitious is merely

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: the shadow of a dream.

speaker: HAMLET
text: A dream itself is but a shadow.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Truly, and I hold ambition of so airy

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: and light a quality that it is but a shadow’s shadow.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then are our beggars bodies, and our monarchs

speaker: HAMLET
text: and outstretched heroes the beggars’ shadows.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Shall we to th’ court? For, by my fay, I cannot

speaker: HAMLET
text: reason.

speaker:
ROSENCRANTZ
              /
              GUILDENSTERN
text: We’ll wait upon you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: No such matter. I will not sort you with the

speaker: HAMLET
text: rest of my servants, for, to speak to you like an

speaker: HAMLET
text: honest man, I am most dreadfully attended. But,

speaker: HAMLET
text: in the beaten way of friendship, what make you at

speaker: HAMLET
text: Elsinore?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To visit you, my lord, no other occasion.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Beggar that I am, I am even poor in thanks;

speaker: HAMLET
text: but I thank you, and sure, dear friends, my thanks

speaker: HAMLET
text: are too dear a halfpenny. Were you not sent for?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is it your own inclining? Is it a free visitation?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Come, come, deal justly with me. Come, come; nay,

speaker: HAMLET
text: speak.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: What should we say, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Anything but to th’ purpose. You were sent

speaker: HAMLET
text: for, and there is a kind of confession in your looks

speaker: HAMLET
text: which your modesties have not craft enough to

speaker: HAMLET
text: color. I know the good king and queen have sent for

speaker: HAMLET
text: you.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To what end, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: That you must teach me. But let me conjure

speaker: HAMLET
text: you by the rights of our fellowship, by the consonancy

speaker: HAMLET
text: of our youth, by the obligation of our ever-preserved

speaker: HAMLET
text: love, and by what more dear a better

speaker: HAMLET
text: proposer can charge you withal: be even and direct

speaker: HAMLET
text: with me whether you were sent for or no.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: What say you?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nay, then, I have an eye of you.—If

speaker: HAMLET
text: you love me, hold not off.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: My lord, we were sent for.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will tell you why; so shall my anticipation

speaker: HAMLET
text: prevent your discovery, and your secrecy to the

speaker: HAMLET
text: King and Queen molt no feather. I have of late, but

speaker: HAMLET
text: wherefore I know not, lost all my mirth, forgone all

speaker: HAMLET
text: custom of exercises, and, indeed, it goes so heavily

speaker: HAMLET
text: with my disposition that this goodly frame, the

speaker: HAMLET
text: Earth, seems to me a sterile promontory; this most

speaker: HAMLET
text: excellent canopy, the air, look you, this brave o’erhanging

speaker: HAMLET
text: firmament, this majestical roof, fretted

speaker: HAMLET
text: with golden fire—why, it appeareth nothing to me

speaker: HAMLET
text: but a foul and pestilent congregation of vapors.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What a piece of work is a man, how noble in

speaker: HAMLET
text: reason, how infinite in faculties, in form and moving

speaker: HAMLET
text: how express and admirable; in action how like

speaker: HAMLET
text: an angel, in apprehension how like a god: the

speaker: HAMLET
text: beauty of the world, the paragon of animals—and

speaker: HAMLET
text: yet, to me, what is this quintessence of dust? Man

speaker: HAMLET
text: delights not me, no, nor women neither, though by

speaker: HAMLET
text: your smiling you seem to say so.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: My lord, there was no such stuff in my

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: thoughts.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why did you laugh, then, when I said “man

speaker: HAMLET
text: delights not me”?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To think, my lord, if you delight not in

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: man, what Lenten entertainment the players shall

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: receive from you. We coted them on the way, and

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: hither are they coming to offer you service.

speaker: HAMLET
text: He that plays the king shall be welcome—his

speaker: HAMLET
text: Majesty shall have tribute on me. The adventurous

speaker: HAMLET
text: knight shall use his foil and target, the lover shall

speaker: HAMLET
text: not sigh gratis, the humorous man shall end his

speaker: HAMLET
text: part in peace, the clown shall make those laugh

speaker: HAMLET
text: whose lungs are tickle o’ th’ sear, and the lady

speaker: HAMLET
text: shall say her mind freely, or the blank verse shall

speaker: HAMLET
text: halt for ’t. What players are they?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Even those you were wont to take such

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: delight in, the tragedians of the city.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How chances it they travel? Their residence,

speaker: HAMLET
text: both in reputation and profit, was better both ways.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: I think their inhibition comes by the

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: means of the late innovation.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Do they hold the same estimation they did

speaker: HAMLET
text: when I was in the city? Are they so followed?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: No, indeed are they not.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How comes it? Do they grow rusty?

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Nay, their endeavor keeps in the wonted

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: pace. But there is, sir, an aerie of children, little

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: eyases, that cry out on the top of question and are

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: most tyrannically clapped for ’t. These are now the

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: fashion and so berattle the common stages (so

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: they call them) that many wearing rapiers are afraid

text: of goose quills and dare scarce come thither.
